$comment: Actio macro layer. This is the ONLY hand-authored schema surface. `scripts/build-schema.mjs` merges it onto the vendored official GitHub Actions workflow schema (vendor/github-workflow.json) to produce actio.schema.json. Edit macros here, then run `npm run schema:build -w actio-core`.

meta.title: Actio workflow (.actio.yml)
meta.description: Source language for the Actio transpiler: the official GitHub Actions workflow schema (inherited wholesale from SchemaStore) plus Actio macro keywords (fragments/inject, retry, dynamic_matrix, fallback) that compile into standard workflow YAML. Every standard GitHub Actions key, event, and value is validated and autocompleted from the upstream schema; the macro keys are additive. See https://github.com/austenstone/actio.

fragments.description: Actio macro. Reusable, named lists of steps. Splice a fragment into a job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

additionalProperties.description: An ordered list of steps to inject wherever this fragment is referenced.

params.description: Actio macro. Typed compile-time parameters. References are resolved with `{{ params.<name> }}` or `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` and stripped from emitted workflow YAML.

job_defaults.description: Actio macro. Default job-level settings merged into jobs at compile time. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

executors.description: Actio macro. Named runner/container/service presets referenced by `jobs.<id>.executor`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

inject.description: Actio macro. Splice in the steps of the named fragment (defined under top-level `fragments:`) at this position. Replaced inline by `actio build`.

executor.description: Actio macro. Name of an entry under top-level `executors:`. Expanded during compile and stripped from output.

default.description: JSON-like default value. Use `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` for string interpolation.

retry.title: retry (Actio macro)
retry.description: Actio macro. Retry a flaky step with optional backoff; expands into a chain of conditional attempts where attempt N runs only if attempt N-1 failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.

delay.description: Backoff between attempts: a duration like "10s", "2m", "1h", or a number of seconds. Injects `sleep` steps between attempts.

fallback.title: fallback (Actio macro)
fallback.description: Actio macro. A native try/catch. Default (notify): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()` but the job still fails. With `recover: true` the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.

oneOf.description: Fallback steps (notify mode). The job still fails.

steps.description: Steps to run when the guarded step or job fails.

recover.description: When true, swallow the guarded failure (continue-on-error) so the job can continue. Step-level only; ignored on job-level fallback.

dynamicMatrix.title: dynamic_matrix (Actio macro)
dynamicMatrix.description: Actio macro. Build a matrix at runtime: `script` prints a JSON array (or `{include:[...]}`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()` with a fail-fast:false default and an empty-matrix guard.

script.description: Command or local script path that prints a JSON array (or `{"include":[...]}`) to stdout.

alias.description: Wrap a scalar JSON array under `matrix.<alias>` (referenced as `${{ matrix.<alias> }}`). Omit for raw `{include:[...]}` mode.

runs-on.description: Runner for the generated setup job. Defaults to the job's `runs-on`, else `ubuntu-latest`.

shell.description: Shell that runs `script` in the setup job, like a step's `shell`. Supported: bash, sh, pwsh, powershell, python. Actio emits matching plumbing to publish the matrix output; other shells (e.g. cmd) are rejected.

compact.description: Pipe the script's stdout through `jq -c .` to normalize the matrix JSON to one line. POSIX-only (bash/sh); ignored for pwsh/powershell/python, which emit raw multi-line output that `fromJSON` parses fine. Defaults to true.

checkout.description: Check out the repository in the setup job. Defaults to true when `script` is a local path.

fail-fast.description: Value for `strategy.fail-fast` on the matrix job. Defaults to false.

id.description: Override the generated setup job id.
